summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Support Life Foundation nonprofit website. All navigation, routing, interactive elements, and page rendering tests passed successfully. No console errors detected.
passed_tests: Home page loads without React crash or errors
All 8 home page sections render correctly (hero, documentary, impact stats, mission story, programs grid, campaign highlights, community impact, international preview, conversion CTA, footer)
Navbar navigation: all links route correctly (Programs, International Programs, Get Involved, Mission)
Logo link routes to home page
Programs page: filter chips work correctly (All Programs: 6 cards, Active: 3 cards, No Longer Active: 3 cards)
Program detail pages: all 4 tested programs load correctly (hot-meals, you-are-not-alone, covid-19-vaccination, millbrae-fire-campaign)
Status badges display correctly for inactive/expired programs
International page: title, 6 pillar cards, and fundraising card render correctly
Fundraising card displays correct amounts ($9,949 raised of $250,000 goal)
Donate page: all 4 donation tier buttons are selectable and update selected amount correctly ($25, $75, $150, $500)
Get Involved page: 3 pathway cards render correctly
Volunteer form with all fields (firstname, lastname, email, message) renders correctly
Mission page: mission statement renders correctly
Scroll behavior: navbar transitions on scroll tested
No console errors or warnings detected across all pages
Images load correctly with fallback handling
Brand colors (blue, yellow/orange, teal) used consistently throughout
Proper spacing, padding, and contrast across all pages
